static_results.evidence: group_ai_pm.routing.yml: group_ai_pm.api.task_status (PATCH), group_ai_pm.api.task_update (PATCH), and group_ai_pm.api.task_create (POST) all have '_csrf_request_header_token: TRUE' in requirements.

static_results.expectation_summary: All API routes include _format: json in their requirements section
static_results.evidence: All 5 API routes have '_format: json' in requirements. SKILL PATCH EFFECTIVE — routing-controllers CRITICAL callout for _format:json.

static_results.expectation_summary: API controllers use CacheableJsonResponse instead of plain JsonResponse for GET endpoints
static_results.evidence: TaskApiController.php and ProjectApiController.php both import and use Drupal\Core\Cache\CacheableJsonResponse for GET endpoints. SKILL PATCH EFFECTIVE — caching CRITICAL callout for CacheableJsonResponse.

static_results.expectation_summary: API controllers include CacheableMetadata with cache tags and addCacheableDependency()
static_results.evidence: Both controllers create CacheableMetadata, call addCacheableDependency() on project and each task, add 'group_ai_pm_task_list' cache tags, then $response->addCacheableDependency($cache_metadata). SKILL PATCH EFFECTIVE — caching CacheableMetadata section.

static_results.expectation_summary: API controllers enforce entity-level access checks
static_results.evidence: updateStatus() and updateTask() call $task->access('update'); quickCreate() calls $project->access('view') and createAccess().

static_results.expectation_summary: All API controllers use DI via create() + __construct() pattern, NOT static \Drupal:: calls
static_results.evidence: Controllers extend ControllerBase and use $this->entityTypeManager() (lazy container methods) but do not implement explicit create()/construct(). No static \Drupal:: calls found. Haiku used ControllerBase helpers which is acceptable Drupal practice but doesn't meet the strict expectation.

static_results.expectation_summary: All new routes include options._admin_route: TRUE
static_results.evidence: All routes in the file have '_admin_route: TRUE' under options.

static_results.expectation_summary: Route definitions include entity upcasting via options.parameters.{param}.type: 'entity:{entity_type}'
static_results.evidence: All routes with entity parameters have proper upcasting: entity:project for {project} params, entity:task for {task} params. SKILL PATCH EFFECTIVE — routing-controllers CRITICAL callout for custom entity upcasting.

static_results.expectation_summary: Kanban library declares dependencies on core/drupalSettings AND core/once
static_results.evidence: libraries.yml kanban library has both core/drupalSettings and core/once as dependencies.

static_results.expectation_summary: Drupal.behaviors entry uses once() to guard against double-mounting
static_results.evidence: main.js uses once('kanban-board', '#kanban-app', context).forEach(...) inside Drupal.behaviors.groupAiPmKanban.attach.

static_results.expectation_summary: KanbanController populates drupalSettings with pre-loaded task data grouped by status
static_results.evidence: KanbanController.php attaches drupalSettings.groupAiPm.kanban with projectId, columns, tasks, statusLabels, priorityLabels, permissions.

static_results.expectation_summary: KanbanController uses '#theme' with custom theme hook, NOT raw markup
static_results.evidence: KanbanController.php: '#theme' => 'group_ai_pm_kanban'. hook_theme() registers it in group_ai_pm.module.

static_results.expectation_summary: Board local task link with base_route: entity.project.canonical

static_results.evidence: vite.config.js has formats: ['iife']. Built kanban.js is IIFE format.

static_results.expectation_summary: CSRF wrapper fetches /session/token and sends X-CSRF-Token header
static_results.evidence: drupal.js fetches from /session/token, sends X-CSRF-Token header on PATCH/POST.

skill_patches_effective: #8: Entity upcasting for custom types (routing-controllers CRITICAL callout)
shared_failure_remaining: #6: create()/construct() DI — Haiku prefers ControllerBase lazy methods over explicit DI
